description.s: Hobson uses an oklch-based color system with CSS custom properties for theming. Styles are items — both kernel styles and user stylesheets.
All colors are defined in the oklch color space, which provides perceptually uniform lightness and intuitive hue manipulation:
— equal steps in lightness look equal to human eyes
— hue is an angle, chroma is saturation, lightness is brightness
The theme is defined as CSS custom properties on
module contains all system CSS. It's a kernel module because styles must load before any rendering happens.
On boot, kernel:styles is injected as a
element. On hot-reload, only the style element is replaced (no full page reload needed).
Userland stylesheets are stored as items with CSS code. The
Views can include scoped styles via
elements in their rendered DOM. These are naturally scoped to the view's DOM subtree when using unique class names.
The import system detects it's a styles module
No full page reload, no re-render needed
